_metadata.description: Ingredient synergy clusters for bonus scoring when complementary ingredients are combined

evidence_tier_definitions.1: PROVEN synergy — combination RCT or systematic review showing >additive effect vs each alone
evidence_tier_definitions.2: SUPPORTED co-nutrients — established biochemical co-dependence or shared pathway with human data
evidence_tier_definitions.3: PROMISING — mechanistic rationale + limited human data, or combo-vs-placebo only (no single-ingredient arm)
evidence_tier_definitions.4: POPULAR combination — each ingredient has individual evidence, but no combo-specific clinical data

_metadata.evidence_source: PMC10600480 (2023 systematic review: Synergistic Health Effects of Dietary Supplement Combinations) + individual PubMed verification

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Piperine inhibits hepatic and intestinal glucuronidation of curcumin, increasing its bioavailability up to 20-fold. Ginger shares overlapping metabolic pathways and may further enhance absorption.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Curcumin Absorption' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: curcumin >= 500, piperine >= 5, ginger >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Influence of piperine on the pharmacokinetics of curcumin in animals and human volunteers
NCCIH Turmeric
Piperine increases curcumin bioavailability by 2000% in humans

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Piperine increases curcumin bioavailability by 2000% in humans (PMID: 9619120). One of the most robustly demonstrated supplement synergies.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Vitamin B6 (as P5P) is required for magnesium transport into cells. Taurine stabilizes neuronal membrane potential alongside magnesium. Glycine acts as an inhibitory neurotransmitter, complementing magnesium's NMDA receptor modulation.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Magnesium Nervous System' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: magnesium >= 200, vitamin b6 >= 10, taurine >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Magnesium and vitamin B6 in stressed healthy volunteers
Magnesium - Health Professional Fact Sheet
Magnesium + B6 for severe stress — Phase IV RCT subgroup

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Mg + B6 for stress: Phase IV RCT (PMID: 30562392) showed 24% greater improvement only in severe/extremely severe stress subgroup. Not superior overall.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Melatonin regulates circadian signaling; magnesium activates GABA receptors and reduces cortisol; L-theanine promotes alpha-wave relaxation; glycine lowers core body temperature to initiate sleep onset.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Sleep Stack' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: melatonin >= 0.5, magnesium >= 100, l-theanine >= 100, gaba >= 100, apigenin >= 50, glycine >= 3000, .... See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Melatonin, magnesium, and zinc supplementation for insomnia in long-term care residents
NCCIH Melatonin
Melatonin + magnesium + zinc for insomnia in elderly — RCT

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Melatonin + Mg + Zinc for insomnia: RCT (PMID: 21226679) showed benefit vs placebo, but no single-ingredient arms to prove >additive effect.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Glucosamine provides building blocks for glycosaminoglycan synthesis; chondroitin retains water in cartilage matrix; MSM donates sulfur for connective tissue repair; omega-3 fatty acids suppress pro-inflammatory prostaglandins via COX-2 modulation.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Joint & Inflammation' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: glucosamine >= 1500, chondroitin >= 1200, msm >= 1000, omega-3 >= 1000. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Glucosamine and Chondroitin for Osteoarthritis
Omega-3 Fatty Acids - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Glucosamine + chondroitin: GAIT trial found benefit only in moderate-to-severe pain subgroup. MSM evidence is weakest. Not clearly >additive.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Lutein and zeaxanthin accumulate in the macular pigment, filtering blue light and quenching reactive oxygen species. Zinc supports retinal metabolism. Vitamin C and E provide systemic antioxidant defense for ocular tissues (AREDS2 formulation basis).
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Eye Health' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: lutein >= 10, zeaxanthin >= 2, astaxanthin >= 4, zinc >= 15, vitamin c >= 250. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Lutein + zeaxanthin and omega-3 fatty acids for age-related macular degeneration (AREDS2)
Zinc - Health Professional Fact Sheet
AREDS2: Lutein + zeaxanthin + omega-3 for age-related macular degeneration

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: AREDS2 formula (lutein + zeaxanthin + zinc + omega-3) tested in 4,203-patient RCT (PMID: 23644932). Established combo for AMD, though lutein/zeaxanthin were a safer beta-carotene substitute rather than >additive.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Methylcobalamin and methylfolate serve as methyl donors in the methionine cycle. TMG provides an alternative remethylation pathway via BHMT. Choline feeds into phosphatidylcholine synthesis. B6 (P5P) is required for the transsulfuration pathway converting homocysteine to cysteine.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Methylation Support' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: methylcobalamin >= 500, methylfolate >= 400, choline >= 250, vitamin b6 >= 10. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Vitamin B6 Fact Sheet
Vitamin B12 - Health Professional Fact Sheet
B12 + folate + B6 for homocysteine lowering — review of RCTs

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: B12 + folate + B6 are co-factors in the methylation cycle. Repleting all three lowers homocysteine more effectively than individual B vitamins (PMID: 36170884). Biochemical co-dependence, not classic synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: NAC is the rate-limiting precursor for glutathione synthesis. Alpha-lipoic acid recycles glutathione and vitamins C/E. Selenium is required for glutathione peroxidase activity. Milk thistle (silymarin) supports hepatocyte membrane integrity and upregulates phase II conjugation enzymes.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Detox Pathway' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: nac >= 600, glutathione >= 250, milk thistle >= 200, selenium >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Selenium Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: L-glutamine is the primary fuel source for enterocytes and supports tight junction protein expression. Zinc carnosine stabilizes mucosal membranes. DGL soothes gastric lining. Butyrate provides short-chain fatty acid fuel for colonocytes.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Gut Barrier' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: l-glutamine >= 5000, zinc carnosine >= 75, deglycyrrhizinated licorice >= 380. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Zinc Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Vitamin C supports neutrophil function and acts as a cofactor for immune signaling enzymes. Zinc is required for T-cell maturation and NK cell activity. Vitamin D modulates innate immune responses through antimicrobial peptide (cathelicidin) expression. Quercetin has demonstrated ionophore activity facilitating zinc uptake into cells.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Immune Defense' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: vitamin c >= 500, zinc >= 15, vitamin d >= 1000, quercetin >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Vitamin A Fact Sheet
Vitamin C - Health Professional Fact Sheet
Zinc - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Vitamin C + zinc for immune support: some cold symptom relief data (PMC10600480), but no RCT proving the combo is better than each alone.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: CoQ10 is an essential electron carrier in the mitochondrial respiratory chain (Complex I to III). Acetyl-L-carnitine shuttles long-chain fatty acids across the inner mitochondrial membrane for beta-oxidation. PQQ stimulates mitochondrial biogenesis. Creatine buffers ATP through the phosphocreatine shuttle.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Energy + Mitochondria' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: coq10 >= 100, acetyl-l-carnitine >= 500, creatine >= 3000, rhodiola >= 200. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Magnesium Fact Sheet
NCCIH Rhodiola

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Ashwagandha modulates the HPA axis by attenuating cortisol release. Rhodiola activates AMPK and modulates monoamine neurotransmitters. L-theanine promotes alpha-wave brain activity without sedation. Magnesium is a cofactor for over 300 enzymatic reactions including those in the stress response.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Stress Resilience' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: ashwagandha >= 300, l-theanine >= 100, rhodiola >= 200, magnesium >= 200. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Magnesium Fact Sheet
NCCIH Ashwagandha

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: 5-HTP is a direct serotonin precursor that crosses the blood-brain barrier. SAMe serves as a universal methyl donor affecting neurotransmitter synthesis. EPA modulates neuroinflammation and cell membrane fluidity in neural tissue. Methylfolate supports BH4 cofactor recycling needed for serotonin and dopamine synthesis.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Mood Balance' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: 5-htp >= 50, sam-e >= 200, saffron >= 30, epa >= 1000. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Folate Fact Sheet
Omega-3 Fatty Acids - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Bacopa upregulates tryptophan hydroxylase and serotonin transporter expression, improving synaptic communication. Alpha-GPC provides choline for acetylcholine synthesis. Lion's mane stimulates nerve growth factor (NGF) production. DHA maintains neuronal membrane fluidity critical for synaptic transmission.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Cognitive Performance' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: bacopa >= 300, alpha-gpc >= 300, lion's mane >= 500, ginkgo biloba >= 120, pqq >= 10. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: CoQ10 supports mitochondrial energy production in cardiomyocytes and acts as a lipid-soluble antioxidant. Omega-3 (EPA/DHA) reduces triglycerides and dampens vascular inflammation. Vitamin K2 (MK-7) activates matrix Gla-protein which inhibits arterial calcification. Magnesium supports vascular smooth muscle relaxation.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Cardiovascular Support' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: coq10 >= 100, omega-3 >= 1000, magnesium >= 200, garlic extract >= 600. See sources for supporting references.

Omega-3 Fatty Acids - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Berberine activates AMPK, increasing glucose uptake independent of insulin. Chromium enhances insulin receptor signaling. Alpha-lipoic acid improves insulin sensitivity and glucose disposal. Cinnamon contains polyphenols that mimic insulin activity at the receptor level.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Blood Sugar Regulation' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: berberine >= 500, chromium >= 200, alpha-lipoic acid >= 300, cinnamon >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Zinc is essential for testosterone synthesis and 5-alpha-reductase regulation. Ashwagandha supports testosterone via cortisol reduction (inverse relationship). Vitamin D acts as a steroid hormone precursor. Tongkat ali may support free testosterone by reducing SHBG binding.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Hormone Balance (Men)' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: zinc >= 15, ashwagandha >= 300, vitamin d >= 2000, tongkat ali >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Vitex (chasteberry) modulates prolactin through dopaminergic activity. DIM promotes favorable 2-hydroxyestrone metabolism over 16-alpha-hydroxyestrone. Black cohosh acts on serotonergic receptors relevant to thermoregulation. Maca supports hormonal balance without directly altering circulating hormone levels.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Hormone Balance (Women)' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: chasteberry >= 160, dim >= 100, black cohosh >= 40, maca >= 1500. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Biotin is a cofactor for carboxylase enzymes critical in keratin synthesis. Collagen peptides provide hydroxyproline and glycine as structural protein precursors. Vitamin C is required for procollagen hydroxylation. Zinc supports keratinocyte differentiation.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Hair & Skin Nutrition' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: biotin >= 2500, zinc >= 15, collagen >= 2500, vitamin c >= 250. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Vitamin A Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Silymarin stabilizes hepatocyte membranes and stimulates ribosomal RNA polymerase, promoting liver cell regeneration. NAC replenishes intracellular glutathione stores. Alpha-lipoic acid chelates metals and recycles endogenous antioxidants. TUDCA protects against bile-acid induced apoptosis.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Liver Support' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: milk thistle >= 200, nac >= 600, alpha-lipoic acid >= 300, tudca >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Selenium Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Calcium provides building blocks; Vitamin D enhances absorption; K2 (MK-7) activates osteocalcin to deposit calcium in bones; Magnesium is cofactor for vitamin D activation.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Bone Health' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: calcium >= 500, vitamin d >= 1000, vitamin k2 >= 90, magnesium >= 200, vitamin d3 >= 1000. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Vitamin D - Health Professional Fact Sheet
Calcium - Health Professional Fact Sheet
FDA denies health claim notification for Vitamin K2 and osteoporosis
Calcium + vitamin D for bone — RCT in vitamin D insufficient women

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Calcium + Vitamin D is one of the most studied combos for bone health (PMID: 18410225). Vitamin D enhances intestinal calcium absorption, but benefit depends on baseline D status.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Vitamin C reduces ferric iron (Fe3+) to ferrous form (Fe2+) at the brush border, dramatically increasing non-heme iron absorption. Folate and B12 are co-factors in erythropoiesis. Lactoferrin facilitates iron transport across the intestinal epithelium.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Iron Absorption' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: iron >= 15, vitamin c >= 250, vitamin b12 >= 50, copper >= 0.9. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: Iron - Health Professional Fact Sheet
Vitamin C - Health Professional Fact Sheet
Vitamin C enhances non-heme iron absorption — systematic review

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Vitamin C converts non-heme Fe3+ to absorbable Fe2+, proven in systematic review (PMID: 10948381). One of the most established nutritional synergies.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Vitamin C (aqueous phase) and vitamin E (lipid phase) form a redox couple — C regenerates oxidized E. Selenium is required for glutathione peroxidase catalytic activity. CoQ10 prevents lipid peroxidation initiation in mitochondrial membranes. NAC replenishes the glutathione pool.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Antioxidant Defense' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: vitamin c >= 500, vitamin e >= 100, astaxanthin >= 4, coq10 >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Vitamin C Fact Sheet
Vitamin E - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Vitamin C + Vitamin E can regenerate each other (C recycles oxidized E). Mechanistic basis is strong, human combo data is moderate.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Creatine replenishes ATP via the phosphocreatine system during high-intensity efforts. Beta-alanine buffers intramuscular hydrogen ions by increasing carnosine stores. Citrulline enhances arginine-nitric oxide pathway, improving blood flow. Caffeine antagonizes adenosine receptors, reducing perceived exertion.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Pre/Post Workout' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: creatine >= 3000, beta-alanine >= 3200, citrulline malate >= 6000, caffeine >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: FDA Questions and Answers on Dietary Supplements
NIH ODS Dietary Supplement Fact Sheets

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Comprehensive enzyme combinations target multiple macronutrient substrates: amylase for starches, protease for proteins, lipase for fats. Betaine HCl supports gastric pH for optimal pepsin activation. Ox bile emulsifies dietary fats for lipase access.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Digestive Enzymes' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: bromelain >= 500, papain >= 100, betaine hcl >= 650. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: FDA Questions and Answers on Dietary Supplements
NIH ODS Dietary Supplement Fact Sheets

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Iodine is the essential substrate for T3 and T4 thyroid hormone synthesis. Selenium is required for deiodinase enzymes that convert T4 to active T3. Zinc supports thyroid hormone receptor binding. Tyrosine is the amino acid backbone of thyroid hormones.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Thyroid Support' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: iodine >= 150, selenium >= 100, zinc >= 15, tyrosine >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Zinc Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Zinc + selenium for thyroid: some evidence of improved thyroid hormone markers together (PMC10600480). Limited RCT data.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Bacopa enhances dendritic branching and synaptic signaling in the hippocampus. Phosphatidylserine maintains neuronal membrane fluidity and supports acetylcholine release. Lion's mane promotes NGF synthesis supporting neuroplasticity. DHA comprises ~40% of polyunsaturated fatty acids in the brain.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Memory & Learning' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: bacopa >= 300, ginkgo biloba >= 120, phosphatidylserine >= 100, lion's mane >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Omega-3 Fatty Acids Fact Sheet
NCCIH Ginkgo

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: L-carnitine shuttles long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for beta-oxidation. EGCG (green tea) inhibits COMT, prolonging norepinephrine-mediated thermogenesis. CLA modulates PPAR-gamma activity in adipocytes. Chromium enhances insulin-mediated glucose disposal, supporting metabolic flexibility.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Fat Metabolism' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: l-carnitine >= 500, green tea extract >= 400, cla >= 3000, caffeine >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Chromium Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Creatine saturates intramuscular phosphocreatine stores for improved power output. BCAAs (especially leucine) activate mTOR pathway for muscle protein synthesis. HMB reduces proteasome-mediated protein degradation. Beta-alanine increases muscle carnosine for pH buffering during intense exercise.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Muscle Building/Recovery' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: creatine >= 3000, leucine >= 3000, glutamine >= 5000, hmb >= 3000. See sources for supporting references.

NIH ODS Dietary Supplement Fact Sheets

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Curcumin inhibits NF-kB transcription factor, reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine expression. Boswellia (AKBA) selectively inhibits 5-lipoxygenase. Omega-3 (EPA) serves as a precursor for anti-inflammatory resolvins and protectins. Quercetin stabilizes mast cells and inhibits lipoxygenase.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Inflammation Control' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: omega-3 >= 1000, curcumin >= 500, boswellia >= 300, quercetin >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Ashwagandha modulates HPA axis signaling and attenuates cortisol hypersecretion. Rhodiola activates stress-protective HSP70 heat shock proteins. Pantothenic acid (B5) is a precursor to coenzyme A, essential for adrenal steroidogenesis. Magnesium is consumed during stress responses and requires repletion.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Adrenal Support' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: vitamin c >= 1000, pantothenic acid >= 100, ashwagandha >= 300, rhodiola >= 200. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Senolytics (fisetin, quercetin) clear senescent cells. NAD+ precursors (NMN, NR) support cellular energy. Resveratrol activates sirtuins. Spermidine promotes autophagy.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Longevity and Anti-Aging' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 2 (moderate). Anchor dose checks: resveratrol >= 250, nmn >= 250, fisetin >= 100, quercetin >= 500, astaxanthin >= 6. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Probiotics modulate gut microbial diversity and competitive exclusion of pathogens. Prebiotic fibers (FOS, GOS, inulin) selectively feed beneficial Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus species. L-glutamine supports intestinal epithelial barrier integrity.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Probiotic and Gut Health' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: probiotics >= 1e+09, saccharomyces boulardii >= 5e+09, inulin >= 5000. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH NCCIH Probiotics Overview
NIH ODS Probiotics Fact Sheet (Consumer)
NCCIH Probiotics: Usefulness and Safety

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Synbiotic concept (probiotic + prebiotic): theoretically the prebiotic feeds the probiotic. However, meta-analysis (PMID: 38999862) found probiotics alone outperform synbiotics.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Sodium, potassium, and magnesium maintain cellular membrane potential and osmotic balance. Potassium citrate supports intracellular fluid volume. Magnesium is required for Na+/K+-ATPase pump function. Balanced electrolytes prevent exercise-induced cramping and dehydration.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Electrolyte Balance' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: potassium >= 1000, magnesium >= 200, sodium >= 500. See sources for supporting references.

sources.label: NIH ODS Magnesium Fact Sheet
Potassium - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Each ingredient in this combination has individual evidence for the stated benefit, but no clinical trial has tested this specific combination together. Classified as a popular stack, not a proven synergy.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Caffeine antagonizes adenosine receptors, increasing alertness and delaying fatigue. L-citrulline enhances nitric oxide production for vasodilation. Beta-alanine increases muscle carnosine for intracellular pH buffering. Creatine provides immediate ATP regeneration during explosive movements.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Pre-Workout Energy' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: citrulline malate >= 6000, beta-alanine >= 3200, betaine >= 2500, caffeine >= 100. See sources for supporting references.

Vitamin C is required for collagen hydroxylation and cross-linking in new connective tissue. Collagen peptides provide structural amino acid precursors (glycine, proline, hydroxyproline). Vitamin A regulates epithelial cell growth and differentiation during re-epithelialization. Arginine supports nitric oxide-mediated blood flow to healing tissue.

Riboflavin (B2) at high dose improves mitochondrial energy metabolism in neurons with impaired oxidative phosphorylation. CoQ10 supports mitochondrial electron transport in the trigeminovascular system. Feverfew inhibits platelet aggregation and serotonin release from platelets.

CoQ10 improves endothelial function and reduces peripheral vascular resistance. Garlic (allicin) stimulates hydrogen sulfide production, a potent vasodilator. Omega-3 (EPA/DHA) improves arterial compliance and reduces systemic inflammation. Beet root nitrates are reduced to nitric oxide via the enterosalivary pathway, directly promoting vasodilation.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Folate (as 5-MTHF) is critical for DNA methylation during oocyte maturation and early embryogenesis. CoQ10 supports mitochondrial energy in oocytes, which declines with age and correlates with egg quality. Myo-inositol acts as a second messenger in insulin signaling and improves oocyte quality in PCOS. D-chiro-inositol restores the myo/d-chiro ratio disrupted in insulin resistance. Vitamin D receptor is expressed in ovarian tissue and supports follicular development.
synergy_clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Fertility (Female)' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: folate >= 400, coq10 >= 200, myo-inositol >= 2000, vitamin d >= 1000, iron >= 15. See sources for supporting references.

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Folate + iron + CoQ10 for fertility: each has individual evidence, CoQ10 for egg quality promising. No combo RCT.

synergy_clusters.synergy_mechanism: Omega-3 fatty acids lower triglycerides primarily via reduced hepatic VLDL production, while niacin raises HDL via inhibition of hepatic lipase and reduction of HDL catabolism. The combination addresses both legs of dyslipidemia more effectively than either alone. Human trial evidence from PMC10600480 showed stronger lipid-related effects with the combo.
synergy_clusters.note: Supported by human trial data per PMC10600480 systematic review. Niacin may cause flushing; extended-release formulations reduce this.

sources.label: PMC10600480: Synergistic Health Effects of Dietary Supplement Combinations
Omega-3 Fatty Acids - Health Professional Fact Sheet

synergy_clusters.evidence_note: Omega-3 lowers triglycerides while niacin raises HDL — addressing both legs of dyslipidemia. Human trial evidence per PMC10600480 systematic review.
